name: Administrative Share Accessed from a Compromised Host
notes: This rule triggers when an administrative share has been accessed from a compromised host.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5001108) Success Audit: A network share object was accessed
when the event matches Share Name (custom) is any of [ADMIN$ or C$]
when any of Source IP are contained in any of Compromised Hosts - IP
TD_list: APPLY Administrative Share Accessed from a Compromised Host on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5001108) Success Audit: A network share object was accessed
AND when the event matches Share Name (custom) is any of [ADMIN$ or C$]
AND when any of Source IP are contained in any of Compromised Hosts - IP
RED_list: Event name: An Administrative share Has Been Accessed From a Compromised Machine
Event description: An Administrative share Has Been Accessed From a Compromised Machine
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
